title: strophanthus caudatus f. undulatus
intro: Strophanthus caudatus f. undulatus is a variety of flowering plant, specifically a type of convolvulus native to tropical Africa, characterized by its wavy-edged leaves and showy, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its cardiac glycosides, which have been found to have medicinal properties in treating heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Strophanthus caudatus f. undulatus include its ability to act as a digitalis substitute, thereby increasing the force and efficiency of the heart's contractions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure. Strophanthus caudatus f. undulatus is typically prepared as a tincture or extract, which can be administered orally or topically, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
